title: Replace podman pause image with rootfs.
body:
This commit removes the code to build a local pause image from the Containerfile and keeps just a code to PullInfraImage.

In case the user-defined infra image is not set, the PrepareInfraSpec is called to find out the catatonit binary and use it using the rootfs with overlay.

This removes the need to build a local pause container image.

The same logic is also applied to createServiceContainer which is originally also based on the pause image.

Fixes: #23292


#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: [NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
I think the failed tests are not related to this PR, because I also see them in the other PRs and they also happen for "main" without any PR.
I suspect it's a race that your PR may have made worse

The

>            "unlinkat /tmp/CI_kDMj/podman-e2e-2902235255/subtest-3653262436/p/root/overlay/03901b4a2ea88eeaad62dbe59b072b28b6efa00491962b8741081c5df50c65e0: directory not empty"

is a red hearing, you see that any basically any log. Yes that is a problem that could be debugged and fixed but it is not what is causing your tests to fail.

The real test issues are logged at the end
```
Summarizing 2 Failures:
           [FAIL] TOP-LEVEL [AfterEach] [Podman play kube with build --build should override image in store](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4781800137949184/html/int-podman-fedora-41-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-play-kube-with-build-build-should-override-image-in-store--1)
           /var/tmp/go/src/github.com[/containers/podman/test/e2e/common_test.go:781](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/1b8cb000dfa469f60cc94cf002cbb58b2590a587/test/e2e/common_test.go#L781)
           [FAIL] TOP-LEVEL [AfterEach] [Podman kube play test with DirectoryOrCreate HostPath type volume and existent directory path](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4781800137949184/html/int-podman-fedora-41-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-kube-play-test-with-DirectoryOrCreate-HostPath-type-volume-and-existent-directory-path--1)
           /var/tmp/go/src/github.com[/containers/podman/test/e2e/common_test.go:781](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/1b8cb000dfa469f60cc94cf002cbb58b2590a587/test/e2e/common_test.go#L781)
```

https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4781800137949184/html/int-podman-fedora-41-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-play-kube-with-build-build-should-override-image-in-store--1

```
Error: unmounting container c1abc2ec7e6d13a83f8b5b18dc26cc4fcefb8ad38af0d9f339f439c6f2b78a54 storage: failed to clean up overlay mounts for c1abc2ec7e6d13a83f8b5b18dc26cc4fcefb8ad38af0d9f339f439c6f2b78a54: unmount overlay /tmp/CI_kDMj/podman-e2e-2902235255/subtest-728196451/p/root/overlay-containers/c1abc2ec7e6d13a83f8b5b18dc26cc4fcefb8ad38af0d9f339f439c6f2b78a54/rootfs/merge: device or resource busy
```

That is new and means something is not right with out cleanup logic, we unmount while it is still busy?

> The
> 
> > ```
> >        "unlinkat /tmp/CI_kDMj/podman-e2e-2902235255/subtest-3653262436/p/root/overlay/03901b4a2ea88eeaad62dbe59b072b28b6efa00491962b8741081c5df50c65e0: directory not empty"
> > ```
> 
> is a red hearing, you see that any basically any log. Yes that is a problem that could be debugged and fixed but it is not what is causing your tests to fail.
> 
> The real test issues are logged at the end
> 
> ```
> Summarizing 2 Failures:
>            [FAIL] TOP-LEVEL [AfterEach] [Podman play kube with build --build should override image in store](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4781800137949184/html/int-podman-fedora-41-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-play-kube-with-build-build-should-override-image-in-store--1)
>            /var/tmp/go/src/github.com[/containers/podman/test/e2e/common_test.go:781](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/1b8cb000dfa469f60cc94cf002cbb58b2590a587/test/e2e/common_test.go#L781)
>            [FAIL] TOP-LEVEL [AfterEach] [Podman kube play test with DirectoryOrCreate HostPath type volume and existent directory path](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4781800137949184/html/int-podman-fedora-41-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-kube-play-test-with-DirectoryOrCreate-HostPath-type-volume-and-existent-directory-path--1)
>            /var/tmp/go/src/github.com[/containers/podman/test/e2e/common_test.go:781](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/1b8cb000dfa469f60cc94cf002cbb58b2590a587/test/e2e/common_test.go#L781)
> ```
> 
> https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4781800137949184/html/int-podman-fedora-41-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-play-kube-with-build-build-should-override-image-in-store--1
> 
> ```
> Error: unmounting container c1abc2ec7e6d13a83f8b5b18dc26cc4fcefb8ad38af0d9f339f439c6f2b78a54 storage: failed to clean up overlay mounts for c1abc2ec7e6d13a83f8b5b18dc26cc4fcefb8ad38af0d9f339f439c6f2b78a54: unmount overlay /tmp/CI_kDMj/podman-e2e-2902235255/subtest-728196451/p/root/overlay-containers/c1abc2ec7e6d13a83f8b5b18dc26cc4fcefb8ad38af0d9f339f439c6f2b78a54/rootfs/merge: device or resource busy
> ```
> 
> That is new and means something is not right with out cleanup logic, we unmount while it is still busy?

It's hard for me to reproduce locally, so I'm going to do few updates here with few tweaks to find out the root issue - mainly trying to use copy instead of bind-mount for /catatonit to find out if the bind-mount is the root cause of the umount issue.

I will probably then do the lsof to find out what's using that mountpoint, not sure if that will be useful or not... :-)

The catatonit bind mount should likely not be the problem. The container mounts should happen in a private mount namespace, so they would not be visible from the host and thus also not cause an EBUSY on removal.


If you like to run losf this could be a good option, trying to add this into the test suite is likely much more complex:

```diff
$ git diff
diff --git a/libpod/container_internal.go b/libpod/container_internal.go
index 05b1b869ec..eea338bb5f 100644
--- a/libpod/container_internal.go
+++ b/libpod/container_internal.go
@@ -10,6 +10,7 @@ import (
        "io"
        "io/fs"
        "os"
+       osexc "os/exec"
        "path/filepath"
        "slices"
        "strconv"
@@ -2056,6 +2057,11 @@ func (c *Container) cleanupStorage() error {
        if c.config.RootfsOverlay {
                overlayBasePath := filepath.Dir(c.state.Mountpoint)
                if err := overlay.Unmount(overlayBasePath); err != nil {
+                       // FIXME: debug only
+                       cmd := osexc.Command("lsof", c.state.Mountpoint)
+                       cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
+                       cmd.Stderr = os.Stderr
+                       _ = cmd.Run()
                        reportErrorf("failed to clean up overlay mounts for %s: %w", c.ID(), err)
                }
        }
```

All tests now pass. The issue was in missing unix.O_CLOEXEC in openDirectory in container_internal_linux.go. This open fd was inherited by aardvark-dns which obviously never closed it and this caused umount to fail with Device or Resource busy.

@Luap99 , I've added `prepareInitRootfs` for the code to find catatonit binary, prepare rootfs and mount-point. I call it from both `generateSpec` and `setupContainer`. The reason is that Rootfs should exist and be configured after setupContainer, otherwise I would have to workaround more conditions which check whether there is some rootfs or image used for a container. I can also do it that way if you think that would be better than calling parepareInitRootfs twice.

I however think it would be somehow fragile. It seems currently, the code just expects that there is rootfs or image configured after setupContainer and I'm afraid changing this presumption could lead to unexpected problems.
